item.title: Why the US Supreme Court struck down Trump’s birthright citizenship order
item.dc:creator: Anagha Jayakumar, Vineet Bhalla

media:content.media:title: Birthright citizenship supporters gather in front of the Supreme Court after a decision on birthright citizenship in Washington, on Tuesday, June 30, 2026. The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down President Trump’s executive order limiting birthright citizenship, reaffirming the long-held principle that the Constitution guarantees that nearly all children born on U.S. soil are citizens. (Allison Robbert/The New York Times)
